content: Use aggregate intent when the user wants totals or counts grouped by a dimension such as family, series, steel, collaborator, location, or knife type. Common phrasings: 'how many by family', 'show the breakdown by steel', 'distribution by location'.

Use aggregate intent with metric total_spend when the user asks how much was spent, what was paid, or spend by period/dimension. total_spend means sum of purchase_price times quantity for inventory rows.

Use list intent when the user wants rows or items rather than a grouped aggregate. Typical phrasings: 'show me my speedgoats', 'which blood brothers do I own', 'list my hunting knives'. Default scope is inventory unless the user explicitly asks for catalog.
Use missing_models when the user wants catalog items they do not own yet. Typical phrasings: 'what am I missing', 'which speedgoats do I still not have', 'models not in my inventory'.

Use compare intent for direct side-by-side comparison across two named periods or entities. If the user explicitly compares two years for spend, prefer compare intent or aggregate intent with year_compare depending on compiler design, but the canonical meaning is a two-period comparison.
Use completion_cost intent when the user asks how much it would cost to finish the collection or buy everything they do not own. This is distinct from current spend.
